title: Trump Witnesses Return of Bodies of 6 U.S. Service Members
description: President Trump traveled to Dover Air Force Base in Delaware to witness the transfer of the remains of five men and one woman killed in an Iranian drone strike.

title: In War’s First Week, a Punishing Military Campaign With No Coherent Endgame
description: The U.S. and Israel have pounded Iran’s leadership and undercut its defense capabilities, but President Trump has offered wildly different explanations for what he hopes to achieve.

title: How D.H.S. Retreated on Immigration Tactics After Minneapolis
description: Agents have conducted more targeted operations, rather than street sweeps, leading to a dip in arrests — at least for now.

title: When DOGE Unleashed ChatGPT on the Humanities
description: Documents show how A.I. was used to cancel most previously approved grants by the National Endowment for the Humanities as the agency embraced President Trump’s agenda.

title: Trump Assembles a New Coalition to ‘Eradicate’ Cartels
description: At a gathering in Florida, the president asked the leaders of a dozen Latin American and Caribbean nations to help the U.S. military crush armed trafficking groups.

title: Punching, Slamming, Screaming: A Chef’s Past Abuse Haunts Noma, the World’s Top-Rated Restaurant
description: Dozens of former employees say René Redzepi inflicted physical and psychological violence on the staff for years.

title: How Candidates Are Using Winks and Posts to Seek Crypto and A.I. Cash
description: All across America, congressional candidates are finding creative — and critics say cynical — ways to signal support for two deep-pocketed industries, A.I. and crypto.

title: How Talarico Won Texas Democrats With Love, Luck and a Little Restraint
description: A carefully disciplined campaign that capitalized on viral media, months of organizing and strong outreach to Latino voters helped propel James Talarico to the center of Texas politics.

title: Economic Warning Signs Pile Up for Republicans Ahead of Midterms
description: With employers cutting jobs and gas prices rising amid the war in Iran, Democrats see an opportunity to press their advantage.

title: A Schools Chief So Charming That Los Angeles Overlooked His Red Flags
description: Alberto Carvalho was seen as a catch for the nation’s second-largest school district. Then his home and office were raided by the F.B.I.

title: There Is One Crucial Reason We’re Talking About Boots on the Ground
description: The U.S. and Israel have no good options to address an unavoidable problem.

title: George W. Trump Goes to War
description: The desire for a revolutionary presidency gives hubris a lot of space to work.

title: Smoking Jars of Metal and Fuses Thrown at Protest Near Mayor’s House
description: Six people were arrested after anti-Islamic protesters led by the right-wing activist Jake Lang clashed with counterprotesters near Gracie Mansion.

title: Communities Survey Devastation After Tornadoes in Michigan and Oklahoma
description: At least six people were dead and well over a dozen injured between the two states after a series of tornadoes on Friday.

title: Epstein Doctor Steps Away From Elite Health Clinics
description: Dr. Bernard Kruger once gave a private E.R. membership to Jeffrey Epstein and unnamed “girls.”

title: Young New Yorkers Decided the Revolution Will Have Roommates
description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s focus on freezing the rent as part of his affordability platform has unleashed a new political force of younger renters.

title: Mideast Fighting: Live Updates: U.S.-Israeli Strikes Grow as Fuel Depots Burn in Tehran
description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the attacks on fuel storage, an expansion of the bombing campaign, were aimed at helping destabilize Iran’s government. Iran’s de facto leader vowed to seek vengeance for the killing of the nation’s supreme leader.

title: Pakistan Praised Trump. Now It Risks Being Caught Up in His War.
description: U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran have set off a crippling energy crisis and sparked deadly protests in one of the world’s most populous Muslim nations.

title: Europe Didn’t Want War With Iran. But So Far, It Can’t Stay Out of It.
description: From London to Rome and beyond, leaders are facing diplomatic headwinds and criticism at home as they take part in a conflict they did not seek.

title: Russia Hits Ukraine With Deadly Barrage of Missiles and Drones
description: At least 10 people were killed when a Russian missile hit a five-story apartment building in the city of Kharkiv, and Kyiv and several other regions also came under attack.

title: How Good Intentions Helped Pave Trump’s Road to Iran
description: Humanitarians proposed a loophole in international law. Decades later, Trump is jumping through it.

title: For Xi, Trump’s Embrace of War Proves China Needs More Power
description: China is learning lessons from the war in Iran. Chief among them, the United States may pose an even greater threat than Beijing thought.

title: Surfers in Munich (Yes, Munich) Just Want Their Wave Back
description: For decades, enthusiasts rode the cresting Eisbach creek in the southern German city, some 200 miles from the nearest coast. But the wave has vanished, prompting arguments about how to restore it.

title: Lebanon at ‘Tipping Point’ as It Seeks to Curb Hezbollah’s Influence
description: The country is waiting to see if the government seizes on this moment to disarm the Iranian-backed armed group and how the militants will respond.

title: He Had a Purple Heart, PTSD and a Rap Sheet. He Had to Leave the U.S.
description: After a long battle with drug addiction, Sae Joon Park felt settled in a new life. But he was deported last year and is now fighting to get back to Hawaii.

title: A Canadian Lawmaker, and Friend of JD Vance, Channels Charlie Kirk
description: Jamil Jivani, a Conservative rookie member of Parliament, follows an American playbook to win over young men on college campuses.

title: Defense Without U.S. Help Is a Live Topic for Canada, Japan and Australia
description: The leaders of Japan and Canada are making a unified front on defense cooperation as President Trump raises the pressure over military spending.

title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.

title: Israel Strikes Oil Facilities in Iran
description: Huge flames erupted into the sky over Tehran and a nearby city on Saturday.

title: Iran’s De Facto Leader Says Iran Will Not Surrender or Stop Its Attacks
description: Ali Larijani, the head of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council and a close confidant of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, said Iran was determined to avenge the killing of the leader.

title: This is what happened on March 7.
Satellite images show U.S. and Israeli targets in Iran.
Deadly Clashes in Lebanon as Israel Renews Search for Missing Airman
description: Dozens were killed in the Bekaa Valley overnight, the Lebanese health authorities said, amid airstrikes and intense violence in the town of Nabi Sheet.

title: Two Toronto-Area Synagogues Are Struck by Gunfire
description: The episodes, which happened late Friday and early Saturday, came after a third synagogue was damaged by gunfire on Monday, officials said.

title: Iran Could Retrieve Uranium at Site U.S. Bombed Last Year, Officials Say
description: The fate of the highly enriched uranium and the options for securing it have become critical issues for the Trump administration.

title: Los Angeles Marathon Runners Can Get a Finisher Medal After Only 18 Miles
description: The decision to award medals to runners who do not complete the entire 26.2-mile race comes as temperatures are set to reach into the 80s on Sunday. Some marathoners disagreed with the change.

title: Immigration Agents Detain a Reporter in Nashville
description: The detention of Estefany Maria Rodriguez Florez, who had sought asylum and is married to an American citizen, raised fears that she had been targeted for her reporting.

title: Democrats, Divided on Past Middle East Wars, Unite Against This One
description: Democrats were torn over military action in Iraq in 2002 and 1991 but are strongly united in opposition to President Trump’s foray against Iran without congressional approval.

title: Seat 11A: The Windowless Inside Joke at 30,000 Feet
description: What looks like a prime spot on the seat maps is prompting disappointment and humor for passengers who find only a blank wall.

title: Trump Calls On Private Companies to Take On a Bigger Role in Cyber
description: In his National Cybersecurity Strategy, President Trump sought an expanded role for private firms in cyberwarfare. He did not take on China or Russia in the document.

title: Appeals Court Upholds Protected Status for 350,000 Haitians
description: The D.C. Circuit ruled against the Trump administration, ensuring Haitians can remain in the United States and keep working while the underlying lawsuit proceeds.

title: State Department Bypasses Congress to Send Israel More Than 20,000 Bombs
description: The sale had been under informal review in Congress, but the State Department declared an emergency because of the war in Iran.

title: Trump Maintains That U.S. Has Ample Weapons as Iran War Depletes Stocks
description: After meeting with defense contractors at the White House, President Trump said the companies were all committed to increasing production.

title: Justice Department Denounces Federal Judges in Fight Against Law Firms
description: The Trump administration had signaled earlier this week that it was ready to abandon four executive orders seeking to punish law firms, but abruptly reversed course the next day.

title: One House Republican in California Retires, and Another Runs as an Independent
description: The decisions by Darrell Issa and Kevin Kiley, made newly vulnerable by redistricting, demonstrated the challenges Republicans face in a difficult midterm environment.

title: Big Tech’s Uncertain Future in the Persian Gulf
description: The expanding war in the Middle East has added new risks to the trillions of dollars in investments that tech companies have planned in the region.

title: War in the Middle East Threatens Global Food Production
description: The Persian Gulf is a major source of fertilizers, making the conflict disruptive to the global production of food.

title: This Work Zoom Shouldn’t Have Gone X-Rated
description: Plus, how to deal when your pal is the toxic one at the office.

title: An Upstart Brewery Sold a Novel Investment to ‘Punks.’ Now They Won’t Get Anything.
description: BrewDog, a Scottish beer company once valued at $1 billion, was recently sold for a fraction of that. Small investors who funded its rise were left empty-handed.

title: ‘Designed to Wreak Havoc’: The Cheap Drones Shaping the War With Iran
description: Iran has launched waves of Shahed drones to menace Persian Gulf nations. The U.S. has unleashed its own copycat on Iran. It’s a sign of how war is changing.

title: Gas Prices Continue to Surge in U.S., Rising 14% in a Week
description: Soaring oil prices suggest that more increases could be in store for American drivers. Diesel, jet fuel, and other refined products are also becoming much more expensive.

title: Divisive F.D.A. Vaccine Regulator Is Resigning
description: Dr. Vinay Prasad drew criticism for overriding career scientists and rejecting drugs by companies seeking agency approval.

title: A Fear for Travelers Escaping the Gulf: Are Flights Safe?
description: Drone and missile attacks have caused high anxiety across the region, but experts say the danger to commercial airliners is “fairly remote.”

title: What to know about the report.
From 2001: Beate Uhse, 81, Entrepreneur in the Business of Erotic Goods
description: She developed Europe’s biggest emporium of erotic goods and opened a three-story sex museum in Germany.

title: From 1973: Mrs. Marjorie Merriweather Post Is Dead at 86
description: The owner of General Foods Corporation, she was a generous philanthropist whose great wealth also allowed her to entertain lavishly and live regally.

title: From 2004: Estée Lauder, Pursuer of Beauty and Cosmetics Titan, Dies at 97
description: Her company, known for its creams, powders, ointments and perfumes, was estimated to be worth about $5 billion when it went public in 1995.

title: From 2004: Julia Child, the French Chef for a Jell-O Nation, Dies at 91
description: She was a towering figure on the culinary front for more than 40 years, turning the art of French cooking into prime-time television entertainment.

title: From 1922: Nellie Bly, Journalist, Dies of Pneumonia
description: She was best known for her trip around the world in 1889, which she completed in 72 days 6 hours 11 minutes.

title: From 2019: Agnès Varda, Influential French New Wave Filmmaker, Dies at 90
description: She was closely associated with the film movement known as the New Wave, although her reimagining of cinematic conventions predated it.

title: Trump Aides Remain Bullish Despite Rising Unemployment and Gas Prices
description: The president’s economic advisers insist that any signs of strains are temporary, as they look to sell the president’s agenda entering the midterms.

title: Democrats Blame Trump for Weak Jobs Report as Republicans Play Defense
description: The weaker-than-expected numbers quickly became a source of tension between the two parties ahead of the midterm elections.

title: Weak Jobs Data Underscores Fed’s Dilemma as War Stokes Inflation Risk
description: The Federal Reserve is still widely expected to hold interest rates steady when its officials next meet on March 17-18.

title: OpenAI’s Fog of War + Betting on Iran + Hard Fork Review of Slop
description: “The Pentagon and OpenAI are saying to the public, You’re just going to have to trust us. And the public is saying, Well, we don’t.”

title: Pentagon Officially Notifies Anthropic It Is a ‘Supply Chain Risk’
description: Anthropic has said it will sue the Defense Department over the designation, which could prevent the start-up from doing business with the U.S. government.

title: S.E.C. Settles Its Case Against Justin Sun, an Investor in the Trumps’ Crypto Coins
description: The decision to settle the court action is particularly striking because the defendant was accused of a serious violation of securities laws. He denied the charges.

title: Meet the A.I. Prospectors Tapping a Billion-Dollar Gusher
description: Brian Janous, a former Microsoft executive, and his firm Cloverleaf have become modern-day land men, packaging electricity and land for data centers.

title: For OpenAI and Anthropic, the Competition Is Deeply Personal
description: A fight over Pentagon contracts shows how the leaders of Silicon Valley’s two most important A.I. start-ups are feuding over the future of the tech industry.

title: A Guide to the Pentagon’s Dance With Anthropic and OpenAI
description: Negotiations, threats and amended contracts have left plenty of questions. Here are some answers.

title: Are A.I.-Generated Videos Changing How We See Animals?
description: By manipulating animals to do wonderful things, we may become numb to their real wonder.

title: From 2017: Frances Gabe, Creator of the Only Self-Cleaning Home, Dies at 101
description: Equal parts quixotic dreamer and accomplished visionary, Ms. Gabe made the house do its own scrubbing.

title: The Smart Home Never Quite Worked. Now It’s Getting an A.I. Reboot.
description: Amazon and Google think that artificially intelligent assistants like Alexa+ and Gemini will speed up the process of setting up a smart home, but many problems remain unsolved.

title: How $800 Monthly Car Payments Are Hurting Car Sales
description: Cars have become so expensive that many Americans are putting off or not buying new cars, hurting the auto industry.

title: You’ve Never Seen Ants Like This Before
description: The Antscan database captures the tiny insects’ diversity and shows how the world’s living things could be rendered in three dimensions.

title: A Word to the Wise: Don’t Trust A.I. to File Your Taxes
description: The world’s smartest technology is no match for the U.S. tax code.

title: Elon Musk Defends Social Media Posts in Twitter Shareholder Lawsuit
description: Mr. Musk said he did not expect his posts in the lead-up to his 2022 acquisition of the social media site, now X, to affect the company’s share price.

title: Trump Announces A.I. Industry Pledge to Pay for Power
description: Companies including Google, Microsoft and OpenAI committed to pay for the power plants and grid upgrades needed to run their data centers.

title: Lawmakers Question Intel’s Use of Tools From Blacklisted Chinese Firm
description: A bipartisan group of senators raised national security concerns about the chipmaker, which is now partly owned by taxpayers.

title: U.S. Automakers Risk Being Reduced to Niche Producers of Gas Vehicles
description: General Motors, Ford and other established automakers risk becoming relics if they don’t catch up to Chinese carmakers and technology companies in electric vehicles and self-driving cars.

title: OpenAI Amends A.I. Deal With the Pentagon
description: The new pact includes additional protections to prevent the use of the company’s technology for mass surveillance of Americans.

title: Reporters Seek Comment. What Happens Next May Surprise You.
description: Accurate journalism requires full and fair reporting. It means pushing hard to hear from those you’re writing about.

title: The Pentagon’s Favorite Tech Guy Is This Hawaiian Shirt-Wearing Founder
description: Palmer Luckey, who founded the defense tech start-up Anduril, has become the It Guy as President Trump aims to modernize the U.S. military with autonomous weapons.

title: A TV Empire Built on Humor and Heart
description: Bill Lawrence, the man behind comedies-with-heart like “Scrubs” and “Ted Lasso,” is in the midst of a career renaissance. He has five shows on the air now, including “Rooster” with Steve Carell.

title: A Maestro’s Fall From Grace Is a Cautionary Tale Worth Heeding
description: Andris Nelsons’s abrupt departure from the Boston Symphony Orchestra shouldn’t be surprising to those who have witnessed his artistic decline.

title: A Washington Museum Zeros In on Presidential Scandal. From 50 Years Ago.
description: The Watergate museum, now in a pop-up phase, focuses on the political crime that brought down Nixon.

title: Bobby Cannavale Loves ‘Heated Rivalry’ and His Bearded Dragon
description: “I spend an hour a day quietly with this guy, whether it’s feeding him, cleaning out the tank, having him chill with me,” the actor said.

title: 8 Songs We’re Talking About This Week
description: Sturgill Simpson’s political screed, Olivia Rodrigo’s Magnetic Fields cover and the Lunar New Year song burning up the charts in Vietnam.

title: When Britney Spears’s Conservatorship Ended, Concern for Her Did Not
description: The pop star’s arrest on suspicion of driving under the influence this week was a breaking point, years after she regained control of her life and finances.

title: Alysa Liu on Dancing Her Way to Gold: ‘The Music Carries My Body’
description: The free-spirited figure skater spoke to our dance critic about her approach to performing, the role of music and more.

title: Top National Symphony Leader Quits in New Blow to Kennedy Center
description: The executive director, Jean Davidson, said her departure reflects frustration at the turmoil that has engulfed the arts center.

title: Timothée Chalamet Has a Point About Ballet
description: This art form is alive, and Chalamet, who comes from a dance family, knows it. But what value does ballet have for the world at large?

title: For the Friars, a Sad Sale of Their Club’s Last Vestiges
description: Friars Club memorabilia, including photos of Billy Crystal and Jack Benny’s violin, sold well at an auction that upset former members of the defunct showbiz fraternity.

title: Meet Kid Harpoon, the Architect of Harry Styles’s Sound
description: The producer and songwriter has been responsible for some of this century’s biggest hits. His greatest skill may be remaining curious.

title: The Unlikely Pairing That Pushed Hip-Hop Forward
description: A Tribe Called Quest had the vision for “The Low End Theory.” The engineer Bob Power helped piece it together.

title: The Funniest Special of the Year So Far Is Drunk on Words
description: Chris Fleming’s wild way with language is both dazzling and hilarious in “Live at the Palace,” an hour that veers in surprising directions.

title: When Hip-Hop’s Wildest Oscar Win Brought Memphis to Hollywood
description: Twenty years ago, Three 6 Mafia’s Oscars triumph for “It’s Hard Out Here for a Pimp” helped an uptight awards show loosen up.

title: Tatjana Wood, Award-Winning Comic Book Colorist, Dies at 99
description: She was part of the acclaimed creative teams on comic book series for DC Comics, including Swamp Thing, which she called “Shvampy” in her German accent.

title: Thaddeus Mosley, Sculptor Who Found Fame in His Last Decade, Dies at 99
description: A self-taught artist, he turned reclaimed wood into striking abstract works influenced by Brancusi, Noguchi and African art.

title: I Make Connections. Here’s What I’m Actually Thinking.
description: The 1,000th Connections puzzle is out today. Wyna Liu, the writer behind the game, knows you have thoughts.

title: The Companionable Brilliance of Helen Garner
description: A newly released collection of the Australian master’s short fiction shows her sympathy, her virtuosity and her ear.

title: Alan Trustman, Who Wrote ‘Bullitt’ and ‘Thomas Crown Affair,’ Dies at 95
description: In a wide-ranging career, he was a Boston lawyer, a Hollywood screenwriter and a Swiss currency trader.

title: Sign Up for the Science Times Newsletter
description: Every week, we’ll bring you stories that capture the wonders of the human body, nature and the cosmos.

title: For These Design Materials, It’s Goodbye and Good Riddance
description: Not all acts of extinction are to be regretted.

title: From 1983: Lise Meitner, Physicist, Is Dead at 89; Paved Way for Splitting of Atom
description: She laid much of the theoretical groundwork for the atomic bomb, although she did not participate directly in its production.

title: From 2000: Hedy Lamarr, Sultry Star Who Reigned in Hollywood
description: A temptress on the silver screen in the 1930s and ’40s, she later became an inventor.

title: Fewer Pregnant Women Received Acetaminophen in E.R.s After White House Warnings
description: A new analysis of prescription data found that emergency room orders for acetaminophen for pregnant women fell for weeks after the federal warning.

title: Feeling the Effects of 260,000 Federal Jobs Lost
description: One year in, assessing budget cuts to federal climate and science jobs.

title: Nature Report, Killed by Trump, Is Released Independently
description: A draft assessment of the health of nature in the United States is grim but shot through with bright spots and possibility.

title: Should New York City Burn Its Parks? This Scientist Thinks So.
description: An unusual outbreak of wildfires in city parks gave scientists a chance to study these rare events. Now they’re coming to different conclusions.

title: F.D.A. Faces Upset Over Denials of New Drugs
description: Agency officials promise fast reviews of new treatments while vowing they will not be a “rubber stamp” for the industry. But patients with rare diseases view recent decisions as signs that the doors are closing on their options.

title: Trump’s High-Profile Oil and Gas Lease Sale in Alaska Has No Takers
description: No companies bid for the chance to drill in more than 1 million acres of water off Alaska’s Cook Inlet, the Interior Department said Wednesday.

title: Most Patients Keep Weight Off With Fewer GLP-1 Shots, Study Finds
description: In a recent review, a small group of patients received the injections less frequently but still maintained weight loss and health benefits.

title: Why Tech Firms Are Working With Trump on Data Centers and Energy Costs
description: The White House has floated a new plan to try to ease voter concerns over the A.I. boom’s effect on electric bills. But it won’t be easy to put into practice.

title: A Nuclear Reactor Backed by Bill Gates Gets Federal Approval to Start Building
description: TerraPower’s project in Wyoming, which uses novel technology, is the first new commercial reactor to receive federal approval in nearly a decade.

title: Sea Levels Are Already Higher Than Many Scientists Think, New Study Shows
description: Researchers found that a majority of studies on coastal sea levels underestimated how high water levels are, and hundreds of millions of people are closer to peril than previously thought.

title: Noem Review Delayed Disaster Aid by Weeks, Senate Report Finds
description: A report by Senate Democrats found that scrutiny of large FEMA expenditures slowed housing and employment assistance for victims of fires and storms.

title: An Iranian Nuclear Site Avoids New Airstrikes, for Now
description: The Isfahan facility, suspected of storing a cache of enriched uranium, was smashed during the 12-day campaign last June.

title: Potomac River Reopens to Some Recreation After Sewer Collapse
description: Contamination levels in the waterway have declined, but health advisories remain in place for boaters downstream.

title: A Danish Program Takes On the Stigma of Mental Illness
description: One of Us, run by Denmark’s health ministry, works with people with mental health conditions to share their stories in schools, hospitals and police stations, helping turn fear into understanding.

title: As Kennedy Turns From Vaccines, MAHA Allies See a ‘Betrayal’
description: As the midterm elections approach, the health secretary is focusing on “real food.” Anger is rising among some of his supporters.

title: States Move to Limit Access to H.I.V. Treatment
description: Citing rising costs and shortfalls in federal support, about 20 states are toughening eligibility requirements for patients in drug assistance programs.

title: Parents Tried to Shield Their Children From Vaccines. Instead They Got Measles.
description: Spartanburg County in South Carolina is ground zero for the largest measles outbreak since 2000. One school has a vaccination rate of 21 percent.

title: Despite Promises, Veterans Affairs Department Cut Thousands of Roles for Doctors and Nurses
description: The Trump administration has eliminated health care positions left vacant after a wave of resignations and retirements at the V.A. last year.

title: They Helped Her Open a Weed Shop. Now They’re Suing for $2.5 Million.
description: A dispute between the nonprofit Housing Works and the owner of a cannabis dispensary shows the risks for businesses in an industry that is locked out of traditional financing and resources.

title: In Sierra Leone, a New Maternal Care Center Aims to be a Blueprint
description: The country has one of the highest maternal mortality rates in the world. Isata Dumbuya, a nurse and midwife, has made it her mission to change that.

title: It’s an Obscure Psychedelic Used to Treat Trauma. Could It Help Me?
description: Veterans and others who have suffered trauma and injuries are flocking to clinics around the world to take ibogaine. My own reason was deeply personal.

title: Elite Doctors Served Jeffrey Epstein While Treating His ‘Girls’
description: A small stable of doctors gave V.I.P. medical services to the sex offender and the women around him. Some doctors bent or broke the ethical rules of their profession.

title: Banks Are Becoming Bulwarks for Vulnerable Seniors
description: Older Americans are losing billions of dollars annually to financial exploitation. Banks and investment firms are training employees to spot red flags and stop the transactions.

title: Iris Cantor, Philanthropist and Art Collector, Dies at 95
description: She and her husband, the financier B. Gerald Cantor, amassed one of the largest private collections of Rodin artworks, donating much of it to museums around the world.

title: C.D.C.’s New Acting Director Draws Unexpected Praise From Agency Staff
description: Dr. Jay Bhattacharya, who was appointed to the role last week, has offered to publicly endorse vaccination, in particular the measles vaccine, winning over some agency employees.

title: ‘The Pitt’ Is Showing Us the Complicated Reality of Psychosis
description: Subtle signs of mental distress often emerge before severe symptoms.

title: New A.C.A. Plans Could Increase Family Deductibles to $31,000
description: The Trump administration is proposing Obamacare plans that it says will lower health insurance premiums. But critics warn they would make care unaffordable.

title: Bird Flu Strikes California Elephant Seals for the First Time
description: Thirty seals, primarily weaned pups, have died since late last week, scientists said.

title: Judge Voids Mass Layoffs at Voice of America
description: The ruling, which said that Kari Lake’s appointment to oversee V.O.A.’s parent agency was invalid, was a major rejection of President Trump’s attempts to dismantle the government-funded news group.
